name: Cheese-Filled Phyllo Pastry (Cigarro Burek)
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Chopped fresh dill, or 1 tsp Dried dill
recipeInstructions:
Throughout the Middle East, bureks are made by folding buttered, cheese-filled phyllo dough into small triangular shapes. Cigarro bureks taste the same but, as the name implies, are rolled into cylindrical shapes resembling small cigars. Bureks, frozen unbaked, can go directly from the freezer to a preheated 375°F oven for an instant hors d'oeuvre.
Makes 40
Defrost the phyllo dough and return the remainder to the freezer. Butter a cookie sheet. Melt the butter and remove it from the heat.
Preheat the oven to 375°F.
Mix the feta, cream cheese, egg, and herbs together in a bowl and set aside.
Place one sheet of phyllo dough on a counter (keep the remaining dough covered with a slightly damp towel to prevent drying). Brush the sheet with some melted butter. Cut it shortways into 5 strips, about 3 x 10 inches each. Place 1½ teaspoons of filling at one end of each strip. Roll the strips into cylinders about ½ inch in diameter. Continue until all dough has been cut, filled, and rolled.
Arrange 2 to 3 cylinders per person on the cookie sheet and brush them with more butter. Freeze the remaining cylinders, unbaked, for use at another time.
Bake for about 10 minutes, or until the cylinders are well browned and very flaky.
